---
title: "STICs"
author: "CLB"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#| include: false
#| echo: false

library(tidyverse)
library(tidygraph)
library(data.table)
library(ggthemes)
library(here)
library(sf)
library(streamDAG)
library(furrr)
library(future)
library(riverdist)
library(sfnetworks)
library(gganimate)
source(here("Code/paths+packages.R"))
library(mapview)
library(cli)
library(whitebox)
wbt_init(exe_path = "~/WBT/whitebox_tools")
# EPSG for NAD83 / UTM zone 14N
UTM_14N <- 26914
source(here("Code/AIMS_DAGs.R"))
source(here("Code/Functions/aims.R"))
source(here("Code/Functions/stic_StreamDAG.R"))
```

# Streams and Sites

```{r}
# knzS <- st_read("~/Desktop/AIMS_GreatPlains.gpkg", layer = "Streams") |> 
#   st_transform(crs = 4326)

# Load sites
gpSites <- st_read("~/Dropbox/AIMS/GIS/Results/AIMS_GreatPlains.gpkg", layer = "Sites") |> 
  filter(watershed %in% c("KNZ", "SHN", "YMR")) |> 
  st_transform(crs = 4326) |> 
  mutate(
    lon = st_coordinates(geom)[, "X"],
    lat  = st_coordinates(geom)[, "Y"]
  ) 

seSites <- st_read("~/Dropbox/AIMS/GIS/Results/AIMS_SouthEast.gpkg", layer = "sites") |> 
  filter(!sublocation == "HW") |> 
  st_transform(4326) |>
  mutate(
    lon = st_coordinates(geom)[, 1],
    lat = st_coordinates(geom)[, 2]
  ) 

mwSites <- st_read("~/Dropbox/AIMS/GIS/Results/GBJ.gpkg", layer = "Sites") |> 
  st_transform(4326)

```

```{r}
# First identify common columns we want to keep
common_cols <- intersect(
  names(gpSites), 
  c("siteId", "slope", "twi", "drainage_area_sqkm", "outlet_dist_m", 
    "elev_m", "watershed", "geom", "lon", "lat")
)

# Process seSites to match gpSites structure
seSites <- seSites |>
  mutate(
    elev_m = elevation,
    slope = slope_point,
    outlet_dist_m = distance_from_outlet,
    drainage_area_sqkm = drainage_area_m / 1000000  # Convert m² to km²
  ) |>
  # Select only the columns we want to keep
  select(all_of(common_cols))

# Process gpSites to match same column selection
gpSites <- gpSites |>
  select(all_of(common_cols))

# Combine the two data frames
allSites <- bind_rows(gpSites, seSites)

# Add missing columns to mwSites, filled with NA
mwSites <- mwSites |>
  mutate(
    slope = NA_real_,
    twi = NA_real_,
    drainage_area_sqkm = NA_real_,
    outlet_dist_m = NA_real_,
    elev_m = NA_real_,
    watershed = as.character(watershed),  # ensure type match
    lon = st_coordinates(geom)[, 1],
    lat = st_coordinates(geom)[, 2]
  ) |>
  select(all_of(common_cols))  # enforce column order

# Bind all sites together
allSites <- bind_rows(allSites, mwSites)
allSites <- allSites |> 
  filter(siteId %in% c("SFM01", "SHM01", "ENM01", "TLM01", "WHM01", "PRM01", "GJ1"))
```

# NASA Power

```{r}
library(nasapower)

get_et <- function(lat, lon, siteId, start_date, end_date) {
  tryCatch({
    et_data <- get_power(
      community = "ag",
      lonlat = c(lon, lat),
      pars = c("EVPTRNS"),
      dates = c(start_date, end_date)
    )
    et_data |>
      mutate(siteId = siteId)
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error for site ", siteId, ": ", e$message)
    return(NULL)
  })
}

```

## 

```{r}
start_date <- as_date("2021-01-01")
end_date <- as_date("2025-01-01")


et_all <- allSites |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  mutate(data = pmap(list(lat, lon, siteId),
                     ~ get_et(..1, ..2, ..3, start_date, end_date))) |>
  select(data) |>
  unnest(cols = c(data))


et_all <- et_all |> 
  mutate(
    ET_mm_day = EVPTRNS * 0.408,
    siteId = case_when(
    siteId == "SFM01" ~ "KNZ",
    siteId == "SHM01" ~ "SHN",
    siteId == "ENM01" ~ "YMR",
    siteId == "WHM01" ~ "WHR",
    siteId == "TLM01" ~ "TAL",
    siteId == "PRM01" ~ "PRF",
    siteId == "GJ1" ~ "GBJ",
    TRUE ~ siteId  # keep other values unchanged
  )) |> 
  select(Date = YYYYMMDD, Site = siteId, ET_mm_day)
```

```{r}
et_all |> 
  write_csv(here("Data/nasaET.csv"))
et_all |>
  ggplot(aes(x = Date, y = ET_mm_day)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~ Site, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(
    title = "Daily Evapotranspiration by Site",
    x = "Date",
    y = "ET (mm/day)"
  ) +
  theme_few()
```
